summary.purpose: Reinforce Unit 1 starting-conversation phrases for the South Wales Entry course.

cards.usage_note: South Wales Entry Unit 1 starting-conversation phrase.

cards.pattern_examples: Bore da / Prynhawn da / Noswaith dda
cards.pattern_examples_en: Good morning / Good afternoon / Good evening

cards.pattern_examples: Bore da / Prynhawn da / Noswaith dda
cards.pattern_examples_en: Good morning / Good afternoon / Good evening

cards.word_breakdown: Noswaith = evening; dda = good after mutation
cards.pattern_examples: Noswaith dda / Nos da / Bore da
cards.pattern_examples_en: Good evening / Good night / Good morning

cards.usage_note: South Wales Entry Unit 1 closing phrase.

cards.word_breakdown: Siôn = Siôn; dw i = I am
cards.usage_note: South Wales Entry Unit 1 self-introduction pattern. Replace the name when speaking.
cards.pattern_examples: Siôn dw i. / Mari dw i. / Twm dw i.
cards.pattern_examples_en: I am Siôn. / I am Mari. / I am Twm.

cards.word_breakdown: Pwy = who; dych chi = you are
cards.usage_note: South Wales Entry Unit 1 formal or plural question.
cards.pattern_examples: Pwy dych chi? / Pwy wyt ti?
cards.pattern_examples_en: Who are you? / Who are you? informal

cards.word_breakdown: Sut = how; dych chi = you are
cards.usage_note: South Wales Entry Unit 1 formal or plural question.
cards.pattern_examples: Sut dych chi? / Sut wyt ti?
cards.pattern_examples_en: How are you? / How are you? informal
